prompt: I want to scaffold a SaaS project management tool. After the initial recommendations, the user accepts them, but then changes frontend from Next.js to Vite + React. Test that downstream decisions (backend, deployment) are properly invalidated and re-evaluated.

assertions.text: Presents an initial recommendation table before any changes
When frontend changes from Next.js to Vite+React, explicitly identifies that backend and/or deployment are affected
Resets affected downstream decisions in the table (back to pending/suggested status)
Walks through invalidated stages to re-decide them with new context (e.g., backend now needed since Vite has no API routes)
Does NOT invalidate unrelated decisions (e.g., database, auth should stay unchanged)
Final table is internally consistent — no decisions that assume Next.js remain after switching to Vite
